mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that suppresses equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC below 0.02, or positive returns occur in >55% of co-declining periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent year-over-year, supply-side expansion supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2 percent fails to generate positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when tested outside mid-cycle expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings erode real corporate profit margins and prompt anticipatory market sell-offs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL values correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.5 million units, the housing sector drag transmits to broader economic activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST values below 1500 fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive returns occurring in >55% of triggered instances or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration, signaling economic weakness that depresses S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance, or positive returns exceed 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, robust real GDP growth offsets rate headwinds by confirming resilient corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with negative returns occurring in >60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.02.
